metadata.description: A collection of Claude Code plugins for language, development, and investment

plugins.name: doc
plugins.description: A plugin for reading and searching various document formats including CHM files

plugins.name: jdk-source
plugins.description: View JDK internal source code for Java development and debugging

plugins.name: korea-gov-data
plugins.description: Extract and organize Open API specifications from KOSIS (Korean Statistical Information Service)

plugins.name: dom-analyzer
plugins.description: HTML/XML DOM structure analysis toolkit with hierarchy visualization and CSS selector discovery

plugins.name: spectrack
plugins.description: Provider-backed workflow over GitHub Issues, Jira, GitHub repository wiki directory, and Confluence with issue and knowledge authoring contracts

plugins.name: obsidian
plugins.description: Generate query blocks, modify templates, and guide plugin usage for Obsidian knowledge management (Dataview, Tasks, Jira Issue, Templater, CLI)

plugins.name: plantuml
plugins.description: PlantUML diagram reference, creation, and validation plugin

plugins.name: structurizr
plugins.description: Structurizr DSL diagram composition, C4 model visualization, and architecture documentation plugin

plugins.name: d2
plugins.description: D2 diagram composition, rendering, and visualization plugin

plugins.name: dev-rules
plugins.description: Gate file edits via a PreToolUse deny + reason that points to the project's coding rule sets (logging conventions; extensible by category) and tells the model to Read them before retrying. Rulesets are organized as `rulesets/<category>/<key>.md`; the hook lists absolute pointer paths (not bodies) in the deny reason so the model Reads the rule files itself, landing them in the transcript as its own tool-result. Per `<category>:<key>` dedup keeps each pointer to once per session.

plugins.name: guard
plugins.description: Enforce evidence-backed technical claims and gate file edits behind explicit user approval. Ships a Grounded output style, logs each session's turns with the assistant's claims and their evidence, audits a turn for grounding on demand (/guard:judge) or automatically each turn in subagent/headless mode, and denies file-mutating tools until the user explicitly approves implementation — in a message or by approving a non-deferring plan in plan mode. The approval gate (edit_gate) and the evidence judge (judge_gate) are set with /guard:config and controlled independently. Requires Python 3.11+. Claude Code only.

plugins.name: hindsight
plugins.description: Mine past Claude Code session transcripts to surface recurring agent mistakes and how the user works, accumulating both as durable, project-independent guidance. Review refreshes the analysis from new sessions; report shows the accumulated findings and profile. Claude Code only.

plugins.name: claude-web-console
plugins.description: Mirror the Claude Code conversation into a local web UI and render fenced code blocks (PlantUML, LaTeX math) as rich output. Streams assistant text live via the MessageDisplay hook and renders diagrams at message completion. Claude Code only.
